source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage - Crane

event.top_event: Hydraulic boom cylinder rod separation from cylinder rod eye
actions_taken: Replaced failed refurbished hydraulic boom cylinder with new unit
Commissioned hydraulic specialty shop to perform in-depth evaluation of cylinders
Conducted metallurgical tests on failed cylinders
Rebuilt both cylinders with matching measurements per original equipment manufacturer specifications prior to reinstallation
event.summary: A hydraulic box boom crane at Castex Offshore's Vermilion 252-A facility experienced two consecutive hydraulic boom cylinder (HBC) failures where the cylinder rod separated from the cylinder rod eye. The first failure occurred two days after installation of a refurbished unit, and the second failure occurred twenty-four days after replacement. Investigation revealed that both failed cylinders had inferior quality welds with voids and lack of penetration, and more critically, the refurbished cylinders had a working stroke roughly one inch different from the originals. This mismatch created applied tension instead of compression, causing rod eye failure. Both failures occurred with little or no hook load and resulted in no personnel injury or additional crane damage.
recommendations: Issue a Safety Alert to oil and gas operators and crane maintenance and repair companies to heighten awareness and prevent reoccurrence
Operators should consider changing both hydraulic boom cylinders when repairs to one are necessary to ensure a matched pair
Alternatively, verify a matched set by confirming the same pin-to-pin centerline (both while collapsed and while extended) and equal working stroke measurements of each HBC before installation

threats.name: Mismatched hydraulic boom cylinder working stroke
Inadequate nondestructive testing of refurbished cylinders

consequences.name: Cylinder rod separation from rod eye
Potential personnel injury from falling load or equipment
Structural damage to crane and adjacent equipment

controls.name: Nondestructive testing (Dye Penetrant) of refurbished hydraulic boom cylinders
controls.barrier_role: Detection of weld defects prior to installation

supporting_text: Both of the failed HBCs had inferior quality welds that went undetected during a nondestructive test (Dye Penetrant) after refurbishment.
Metallurgical tests conducted post incident showed the first failed HBC to have 'a poor weld with multiple voids and a lack of penetration'

controls.name: Verification of matching hydraulic boom cylinder specifications
controls.barrier_role: Ensure dual parallel cylinders have identical working stroke and pin-to-pin centerline measurements

supporting_text: the refurbished cylinders had a working stroke roughly one inch different from the originals
Failure to recognize the importance of ensuring the same pin-to-pin centerline (both while collapsed/while extended) and equal working stroke measurements of each HBC
the combination of the under welded rod eye and difference in working stroke would result in applied tension (torque) instead of compression and cause rod eye failure

controls.name: Dual parallel hydraulic boom cylinder design
controls.barrier_role: Redundancy to maintain boom control if one cylinder fails

supporting_text: The crane is equipped with dual, parallel HBCs that are designed to work together to raise and lower the box boom.

controls.name: Replacement of both hydraulic boom cylinders when one requires repair
controls.barrier_role: Ensure matched pair operation to prevent torque-induced failures

supporting_text: operators should consider changing both HBCs when repairs to one are necessary to ensure a matched pair
An alternative to changing both would include verifying a matched set

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
